major: History of Art And Visual Culture B.A.

groups.group_label: Complete 3 semester units from the following

courses.title: Colonial Histories and Legacies: Africa, Oceania, and the Indigenous Americas
History of Art: Arts of Africa, Oceania and Native North America
History of Art: Arts of Africa, Oceania and Native North America

notes: All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website .

This is a non-screening major: completion of specific major coursework is not required for admission. However, students are strongly encouraged to complete as many lower-division courses as possible from the " Recommended Preparation " section below to support timely degree completion. Students applying to non-screening majors are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements prior to transfer. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor.

The History of Art and Visual Culture (HAVC) program at UC Santa Cruz welcomes transfer students. Although not required for admission, students are encouraged to complete up to three of their lower-division HAVC requirements prior to transfer, each selected from a different geographic region: Africa and its Diaspora Asia and its Diaspora Europe and the Americas Mediterranean Native Americas Oceania and its Diaspora HAVC 80 – Colonial Histories and Legacies: Africa, Oceania, and the Indigenous Americas* *HAVC 80, or an articulated equivalent, may be used to fulfill a lower-division requirement for one of the following geographic regions: Africa, Native Americas, or Oceania. In the "Recommended Preparation" section below, you will find lists of courses approved to fulfill these requirements. Petitioning Courses: Transfer students who have completed courses they believe are applicable to the geographic regions listed above, but are not listed in the "Recommended Preparation" section below, may petition to have these courses counted toward their HAVC major requirements. Students should consult the Transfer Credit Petition Process instructions on the HAVC Department website . Students are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor. Please visit the History of Art and Visual Culture website to learn more about this major.
